review_text: Magnificent location for caravanning must be the best in Australia. The site and personnel are fantastic. Obviously from the condition of the park management put money back into maintaining and improving the park, not just taking.
Beautifully maintained premises with security, green lawns, fantastic play equipment and more.
My boys love the park so much to do, they especially loved bingo with Sam @ my youngest loved decorating cookies with Georgie
One of our most favourite places in the world, couldn’t recommend more highly
Great park, we've travelled around Aus and this ranks among the best
Great park
Got everything here, don’t need to go anywhere else. Great for kids with the variety of swimming pools & play grounds. Even had a night outside TV for those special get togethers

Love the caravan park but not the local thieves. Had 2 e bikes stolen from our site that were chained to our caravan a frame. Cut the chain with bolt cutters. Reported to Henley police who told me that this was a common occurrence at this park. Next year will have better locks with audible alarms!

An amazing centre with everything you could ask for and more, including good sized powered sites, and friendly and helpful staff right from the booking process. Only draw back is planes and helicoptors fly well into the night (2am was the last one I heard) and start again at around 5am. Bring some earplugs and you and your family will have an incredible holiday.

A busy park,but plenty of space and amenities for families. Children enjoyed all the swimming and play areas. Great coffee at 'The Shack' cafe - love being able to order from room for quicker pickup!

Conveniently located next to the beach. Stayed in the Flying Fish room - a little run down but ok if you dont plan to be in it all day and adventuring instead!

Conveniently located near the putput golf and the surf life saving club.

A fantastic family friendly park.They boast about good coffee in their cafe, and they are right, it is actually good!
The facilities are so well considered. We came for a school holiday "staycation", there have been many good activities for the kids, movie and entertainment.
The park was full for Gather Round, but with abundance of staff and facilities it was like nothing went amiss.
Staff have all been friendly and helpful. The activities like craft, mini golf, driving range, bingo - all very popular but the park is mostly well set up for the crowds and manage it well.

The only downside, which is out of the parks control is the planes start at 6am, one after the other taking off. This doesn't bother my kids, but wakes me.
We've been to the park several times already and we will be back again.

Incredible caravan park, great facilities for families. You realistically don’t have to leave the park once you’re there as they have everything to keep the kids amused for days. We had a run of bad weather and the kids were still really happy at the park. It is very safe and in a wonderful location. Easy walk to minigolf and also the West Beach adventure park for the high ropes course. Can be sensitive to the wind off the coast which can affect use of caravan awnings etc. We throughly enjoyed our stay.

Great park for families. Plenty of activities for the kids. Easy access to the walking path along the beach.
We stayed in Willy the Whiting cabin and it was clean and modern.
It’s a large caravan park but plenty of space between cabins and caravan sites.
Campsites are exce, good size. Toilet blocks need a refresh. The old terrazzo floors are cracked and tired.
I loved The grass and the mini golf course was a nice activity.

Our stay here was spoilt by thieves who stole our friend's two electric bikes. Both bikes were secured with chain locks. You might say this has nothing to do with the caravan park, but a visit to a local bike shop soon uncovered scores of their customers who had suffered the same fate over a long period of time.
It is not good enough for the caravan park to allow this to happen over an extended period of time, without letting guests know of the risk and encourage them to secure their valuable assets.

It’s saddens me to know that there has been multiple bike theft incidents at this park and that not one staff member or any signs to inform campers .
We had a wonderful time at west beach caravan park and the facilities were very good.

On our last night we had our 2 foldable e-bikes stolen from our caravan , they cut the safety chain lock and hiked off with them.

We thought this caravan park had good security but it seems it doesn’t as one bike shop has had 6 complaints about there bikes being taken .

We can’t afford to buy new ones and our insurances are not looking like they will be covered .

If we had been notified when checking in or there were signs around stating the recent theft’s we and others could have done more to protect them.

In happy camper

Westbeach is our goto caravan park to spend time with family and friends.
As a neurodiverse family, staff are always accommodating and the park has a supportive and safe atmosphere.
We stayed on 13th ave which was a sensational spot. The kids absolutely loved the new pool and we took advantage of the huge kitchen area in the hub. Great spot. We love West beach.
Mini staycation with my grand daughters.  Amazing place to stay!! So much to do just on the site from swimming to movies and more.

Immense camping parfaitement entretenu, tout est propre et bien organisé. Beaucoup d’activités autour pour occuper toute la famille, l’endroit est agréable et vivant sans être oppressant. Très bon niveau d’équipements, on s’y sent tout de suite bien et on profite pleinement du séjour.
Loved it here, absolutely great for kids will definitely be coming back here

I'm staying in a chalet. They have  chalets with on suites but I'm staying one without. Very nice & comfortable . No hangers in cupboards. Good ammenities, coffee shop& shop for all your needs. There is so much for children activity centre, swings, swimming pools & much more. There is also adult areas. Barbecues, laundrey. For such a large park it is very quiet. Basically you would not need to leave the park to find all your needs. I thoroughly recommend.

JUST NOT RELAXING. Don’t bank on a sleep in. Not the place to be if you don’t have kids. Kids everywhere, running through your sites, yelling screaming no sh!ts given, but they’re having a great time. If you’re after a quiet relaxing holiday destination look elsewhere. Wasn’t expecting this outside of school holiday period. On a positive it has good security, ammenities are tidy. Summary: If u don’t have kids DON’T just DON’T come here, if u do this is the park to entertain your kids.
Great facilities and great service. Loved staying here.

Stayed 2 nights with 4 adults +4 kids in 2 cabins and we absolutely loved our time at West beach discovery park! So many activities for the kids. Rooms were clean and  prices for snacks and food in kiosks were so reasonable. Will definitely  be back again!
Friendly park. Loads of kids activities. Top location. The best.

I cannot express enough how exceptional Carly’s customer service was.

We were staying for 7 nights at Discovery Park and, due to some very unexpected and exceptional circumstances that meant we were unable to return to Victoria on our checkout day, we urgently needed to extend our stay by one additional night.

We went back and forth both over the phone and in person with other staff members throughout the day, but unfortunately no one was able to help us.

Later that afternoon, we spoke with Carly at reception and she went absolutely above and beyond to accommodate us. With very little hassle, she managed to fit us in for the extra night and completely turned what had been a very stressful situation around.

I only wish everyone was as empathetic, understanding, and accommodating as Carly. She is an absolute asset to your team — give that woman a pay rise!

Unsafe, uninhabitable accommodation – refusal to refund.
We booked and paid for accommodation at BIG4 in good faith. Upon arrival, the cabin was not fit for occupation, particularly given extreme heat and the presence of two young children.
The air-conditioning was not operating to a level that made the cabin habitable, and there was no visible evidence of current electrical test and tagging on appliances. These issues were raised immediately with management. After consultation, it was clear the issues could not be rectified within a reasonable timeframe.
Given the conditions, we had no practical or safe alternative but to leave and secure alternative accommodation at short notice and additional cost.
Despite this, management refused a refund. The written response we received from the Holiday Park Manager stated:
“There will be no refund for your cabin as the cabin was checked on your departure day with everything working fine in there. Have a nice day.”
This response is dismissive, inconsistent with our lived experience, and fails to acknowledge basic consumer and safety obligations. Under Australian Consumer Law, accommodation must be of acceptable quality and reasonably fit for purpose. A failure of essential services in extreme heat constitutes a major failure, entitling a consumer to a refund.
We are now escalating this matter to Consumer and Business Services (SA) and SafeWork SA for review of both consumer compliance and safety standards.
This was a deeply disappointing experience, not just because of the condition of the accommodation, but because of the unprofessional and legally flawed response from management when the issue was raised.
Until this is addressed appropriately, we cannot recommend staying here.

We were here last week and had to be separated with our other family members because they have dogs. We saw 2 different sites with a dog each and a cabin with a dog also. Is this caravan park is a NO DOG at all or on certain months in a year only. The park itself is just amazing for family with kids and couple just want to rest and relax. Will definitely return again ;)

Hands down best park we’ve ever stayed at enjoyed it so much we ended up booking an extra night
Amazing stay for our little family staycay for the husbands birthday and amazing sunset views
And such nice extra touches, balloons and chocolates in our cabin on arrival for hubbys birthday
We only live 15 minutes down the road and will def be back for another getaway 😊✌️

2 night stay . Haven't been here for almost 20 years and boy has it changed. I didn't have any children with me on this occasion but there is so much for kids , playground, pool , bike/ skate park, outdoor cinema., lots more to do with all age children. Plus the beach . Close to other areas like Glenelg. Bus services right outside the front gate .
A cafe / shop inside the park and another just on the side .
Absolutely amazing resort especially for younger kids plenty to do lots of activities, facilities and plenty of shade so hot days aren’t an issue. Second time here and we love it

Jets fly overhead 24/7, sewage outflows on the beach. Overpriced. Shared bathrooms locked down while we were there due to ringworm infection and cleaning. Laundry stolen
Worst caravan park ever seen

I was very much looking forward to my 2 night stay in West Beach Caravan Park , but the greeting to the park wasnt a positive one , they couldnt find my name , booking etc..it made me feel uneasy , it took quite a while for them to find my booking..The cabin was very old , deteriorated & in urgent need or a revamp..the mould in the bathroom & main bedroom was a worry. The actual facilities of the park were great.

Umm UNREAL! Has everything you could ever want. Pool, toddler pool (absolutely perfect), splash park, mini golf, gym, kids activity room, beach at your door step! We had a grass caravan site and the maintenance men worked non stop to keep this park beautiful. Will be back! 🙌 - Abbi & Ryan Walker

Amazing spot!! We loved our time at west Beach and have already rebooked for next year. Highly recommended with great pools, movie nights and plenty of kids activities. Plus the beach is right there!!! Water slide park, mini golf and high ropes course is next door

Great park with heaps to do for the family great location only down fall would have to be the amenities blocks they are outdated and could benefit from a make over. Also the laundry’s are the same several machines out of order throughout the park at what is there busiest time of the year all in all a great park and we will be back for the 4th year in a row at the end of the year. Staff are great and all so helpful and willing to help where needed. Thanks team and see you again in December.

Lovely and clean. Beautiful grassed camp sites   No lawless children in places other than pool and playgrounds.
Family friendly but also quiet, safe and comfortable for grey nomads and romantic couples to enjoy their holidays and romantic getaways.

This park was great but it was dampened by the smell of cigarette smoke blowing into our caravan and with our baby inside asleep at night it was really horrible.
A woman was chain smoking on her cabin verandah nearby...and another man lit up in a site adjacent to us and was extremely respectful and put it out after I asked, but this park should be completely smoke free.
It is marketed as a family park but there is nothing family friendly about allowing smoking within 2 metres of where children are playing, eating and sleeping...
West Beach Parks is by far the best park I've ever stayed at. It's facilities for kids with the new toddler pool, activities and site (for caravan) were incredible.
But smoking should not be allowed. If this park became smoke free it would probably be one of the best in Australia.

Great caravan park, one of our favourites. So close to everything you would forget you are only half an hour from home. So self contained you only have to leave for more food or evening walks along the beach. Cafe, swimming pool, splash park, toddler pool, multiple playgrounds, outdoor cinema, basketball courts, kids club, super clean amenities and more. Just a short walk to West Beach Mini Golf and the new 'The Square' which houses a huge playground picnic area, the Adventure Park Mega Climb and The Big Wedgie.
Always a pleasure to stay here.
My rating is based on our visit to friends who were staying there , what we saw and what our friends told us about the park.
Great caravan park, perfect for young kids and families. So much to do in the park you'll forget you are right next to the beach! 10/10 would stay and will be staying again!
Not pet friendly even though they say they are part of the discovery parks franchise
Fantastic park. Central to everything and great facilities

Nice place to stay. Short walk to the beach. The pool was awesome and great playgrounds for the kids, as well as lots of activities for them to do.

Only downfall was the laundry facilities. A few dryers out of order and the ones that were working took a long time to dry.

Overall we loved our time here and would stay again.
Will always come back.

We stayed in a standard cabin, great location, helpful staff, lots of kids activities, plenty of food and drink avaliable around the park, clean central amenities
Well set out, Highly recommend.
Kids running around at 1 am fireworks and planes never again not even school holidays

Very clean and tidy, good size caravan sites, friendly and helpful staff and so many free kids facilities like 2 playgrounds, pool, jumping pillow, daily activities like craft, cookie decorating, quiz nights and bingo plus a movie every night on the big screen. Perfect location to base yourself to explore Adelaide and surrounds including the hills. Highly recommend.

We stayed for a few nights on an ensuite site. The campsite was huge and beautifully situated, but the ensuite itself was tiny, outdated, and uninviting. There were plenty of entertainment options for children, which was very nice. The pool was refreshing and a welcome bonus. With some effort in renovating and maintaining the old public amenities and the ensuites, the campsite could easily earn five stars.

Un immense camping situé en bord de mer, avec un accès direct à la plage. Tous les accès au camping sont sécurisé par code. Les emplacements sont spacieux et recouverts d'herbe. Ne nombreux équipements sont installés sur place dont un écran de projection extérieur et une piscine. Nous avons grandement apprécié notre séjour

We stayed three nights. I don't know why this park is so hyped, to be honest.
The ensuite sites are tiny and old. Our little one burnt himself when washing his hands. No-go. Water temperature needs to be regulated. The shared bathrooms are no better. All dated and not very inviting. There is just one camp kitchen in the park and the park is huge. The other area labeled camp kitchen can hardly be called one. There is nowhere to sit or cook, unless you are ok with just a microwave, a kettle and toaster. The playgrounds are nice but there is great ones in the city, too. There is going to be a toddler pool soon,  it's under construction. We wanted to check in at 1pm but were told our ensuite is not ready. We could still do the check in and park on our site but had to go back to get the key at 2pm. The key has already been there when we checked in. There was noone cleaning between 1pm and 2pm, so I don't really get what that was all about. Same thing happened to our neighbors.

The caravan park is fine, but very busy. We had a poor experience with a phone receptionist. We had a medical appointment, and I rang to see if we could check in about an hour early, to save us trying to find a park near the doctors big enough for our car and caravan. The receptionist was unhelpful and very inflexible. She said we might be able to check in early, but she couldn't guarantee that. Then told me we could leave our van across the road in an unmonitored general car park, (no designated caravan parks there). Then she told me we could book the site for the previous night, even though we were not going to be in Adelaide to use it. We have been using the cabins and caravan sites for over 20 years but that is the last time. We will not be back.

This park is absolutely huge. When we arrived we thought we'd made a mistake booking here as we are older. Kids everywhere.  When it came time to leave I didn't want to go. Loved it. Bus at the gate to Glenelg or into Adelaide.  Absolutely perfect. Huge sites. Every possible thing you could want. Definitely would go back.

A caravan park for kids and unsuitable for mature adults. Staff clean kitchen in the peak time, woken up to a construction site operating early with machinery. Ok to build nice toddlers’ pool but what about equipment not working in the kitchen or broken and missing tiles at the urinal ?|I have been here before and the advantage is the sea breeze.

This place is huge, for a change im in the cabins ( normally roof top tent), cabins are very clean very comfortable with air con and tv.
Park has a decent sized pool, good dinning areas and BBQ's
Good clean toilets and showers and good sized Laundry
Lots for kids also
Its located along the beach through a locked gate youll have a swipe card for access

We stayed on a powered camp site. Camp sites were large and well kept grassed. The park itself was huge with lots of different facilities and beach access. Bathroom was standard and quite large. Kiosk was available and sold alcohol and snacks.

I really appreciated our recent stay at the park. The cabin was clean and had everything we needed. We ended up near the beach access which was nice. Plenty of amenities in the park and a great location as well. Will stay again!

Nice park for kids but the amenities are atrocious and should be completely renewed.

The coffee at the Shack is watered down and tasteless.
We stay there on every trip to Adelaide even if it's not on our way. Staff are so helpful & facilities are spotless clean. Short walk to beach & the SLSC which has a very tasty menu.
Great caravan park
Great place for plane spotting
As a couple the location is great close to shops and airport. If we were a family with kids it would be our go to park. It offers everything to entertain families
I could just relax there and dont leave

This park has everything you could want.  Apart from large sites for everyone and clean modern amenities there were four coffee shops and four different types of playgrounds for all ages and abilities.  A sensory room, BBQ, fire pit, movies, craft room, free bus to shopping outlets bingo, free yoga, golf, etc etc. Talked to families that live in Adelaide and save to bring family here for holidays it's so good!
Love visiting here when we come to SA to visit family as the grandkids can come play lots to to do.

Nice park but cabin, coral#3 was very expensive for a very budget cabin in need of maintenance, our sliding door hardly slid and the screen door fell off twice, there were holes in the walls and bed was terrible, amenities were not cleaned well, and the 10pm quiet rule was not observed we had teenagers running wild around our area till well after midnight which was annoying with children trying to sleep, the pool, playgrounds, and cafes are all excellent but I would not stay in a cabin in this park again!

We holiday here from Melbourne every spring after finding it by accident 3 years ago. It has a few BBQ areas, a cafe inside the park, 3 playgrounds of different styles one massive one with a double flying fox, 0-1.8m gradual depth pool with fountains perfect for the kids, a jumping pillow, an arcade room, a communal fire pit, as well as some gas heaters around outdoor tables and this year a new huge outdoor cinema which also had the kids morning cartoons and music videos for the 'downtime' The best part though is the kids club and other activities each day like, bingo, trivia, taekwondo, cooking, and crafts classes and many others all organised by "Miss Deb" as my kids call her. We easily stay here for 2 weeks without leaving the campsite unless we want to walk to the mini golf, adventure climbing or beach that are all next door. It's a nice big campsite that's well kept, full of trees, bright green grass and modern clean buildings and facilities.

Great location, beach access from the back of the park.
Great facilities for families.
Great family oriented caravan park with plenty of amenities to amuse adults and children alike. There has been plenty of investment in keeping the park neat, tidy, and up-to-date
Stunning park! Awesome amenities, awesome kids facilities (multiple playgrounds around the park and a great pool). Highly recommended!!! We will be back for our 4th visit next year!

Huge Family Park
Clean in great seaside location

stayed in on of the cabins at the back. rooms were a little dated but still fine. showers were updated and worked well.
AC was quick to do the whole apartment
This is the best park I have been too. It is so well set up and so much for the kids to do.

Located on the beach this caravan park has been located here for many years. We experienced a busy night last night and it was enjoyable. There are lots of activities and the facilities are clean and there is so much to do.
Absolutely loved our stay and experience. Kids had the best time and we, adults, also had heaps of fun. We’ll definitely going back
Great park and facilities

Everything you need for all ages .Neat,tidy,
Playgrounds and pool. Beach at your door. Walking tracks along the beach.
Raised lookouts for Sunset photos.
Food drinks and takeaways right in the park .
Restaurants and pubs close by and Westfield Westlakes 15 minutes .
15 minutes to Adelaide City Centre.
7 minutes to the Adelaide Airport
and huge Brandsmart .

A great place for families and close to Harbour town. The shack cafe is ok but our fish and chips were ordinary as the chips were almost cold and not freshly cooked. The fish upset my stomach to the point where I nearly vomited. The laundry facilities need fixing as there's only a couple of machines working and others not working the whole time we stayed there. It's not really good enough with the amount of people staying at the park and how much it costs to stay. Obviously maintenance is not high on their agenda! I would consider not staying here again in the future.
Cannot recommend this park highly enough. BEST park we have stayed at in our caravan. The facilities are wonderful. SO much to do for kids. Can’t wait for our next visit in October school holidays!!
Nice park, weather was rough

Unhelpful and patronising staff member/s that treated us Royal Show competitors like second class citizens. Rude and unhelpful .. preferred to refund entire trip than try to help us when problem arose after a very long trip and day.
Used to stay here every year, will not be staying again and will recommend others elsewhere.
Always love coming here with the family. Kids have so much fun with so much to do. Playgrounds, flying fox, pools, Cafe, beach, activity center, gaming room.

This park is very clean and tidy. The powered site for our RV had an ensuite (which worked well) and it was a roomy site.  Shame about the crazy weather, but the park looks like it has heaps of facilities for a family.  It is very close to the airport which is handy and not far out from the central city.
Excellent, clean and plenty for children to be entertained. Recommend.

Great park with unlimited facilities for children and adults alike.  Right on the water but rates are significantly lower than other regions of Australia!!  Facilities are kept in good order even with the significant child traffic.  Walking distance (5 min) to the West Beach Surf Club.  Well done West Beach!!
Fantastic park! Soo much for kids to do. Park and bathrooms are kept immaculate. Right on the beach
Very nice clean place

Superb caravan park. We stayed in our van and a friend had a cabin. The cabin was neat, clean and well equipped. Spacious van sites with some drive-throughs. Clean amenities, bathrooms for disabled, laundry. Wonderful facilities for children - pool, jumping pillow, activity room and playground. The park has secure fencing all around and provide swipe cards for gate access and access to bathrooms. The park is right beside the beach. Ten minute drive to Harbour Town and there is a courtesy bus from the park at 10am to Harbour Town each Wednesday morning returning at 1pm. Small coffee shop just outside the park with a small variety of groceries, milk etc. and gas bottle exchange.
We've had a cracking stay and would definitely stay again.

We were blown away by how spotless this park was, and it had all the bells and whistles our massive van needed for four whole nights. The fact that it was right on the beach was basically the icing on the cake. And all the staff were amazing.

We had the absolutely most incredible time at West Beach! The park is so lovely! Very well maintained & the facilities were fantastic! There is so much for kids to do & everything is so well maintained! The staff were incredibly friendly! While the bathroom blocks were older & in need of repairs they were always spotless! We will absolutely be back!
Amazing place... love it

Amazing place to go with big groups.||We went with several other families, the holiday park did their best to group us all together. Our kids had so many things to do and although we were litteraly on the beach the kids didn't want to leave the holiday park with things to do for kids of all ages.||Minor things like a tap that would leek constantly and a small battle with ants.... it didn't affect the quality of our stay.||Will definitely come back!

We came down for a family getaway, stayed in a family cabin - Sally the Seashorse #9, for the price for 4 nights for 4 people of $960+ the facilities were good except some facilities towards that area of the park would have been nice, ||The stay was far from the getaway we were hoping for with the cabin been cold on the first night we decided to put the split system on to heat the room, it wasn't very warm so we checked the air-conditioner filters they looked like they hadn't been cleaned for months so we cleaned them and replaced, the dust on the headbord in the main bedroom and on top of the cupboard made for an interesting time with wifes asthma, bed mattresses were like sleeping on concrete,  kids bunk area had dirty marks on the ceiling and old mentos stuck under the mattress bunk mattresses were uncomfy with one child sleeping on the lounge, shower was more cold then hot, and had black mold along the seals, no extra blankets in main bedroom for chilly night temps either||When told about the aircon on the second day we were told housekeeping would attend to it while we were out - didnt see house cleaning until sunday and they didn't clean our cabin

Great experience.
Facilities were good and it's extremely family friendly.
The location is beautiful.
Short drive from shops and about 20 minutes from Adelaide.

Had goods stolen from inside our vehicle and also a bike parked under our caravans awning. Learnt afterwards that apparently this is common at this park. Would have appreciated to be warned at check-in. Hopefully this review will help others be better aware of this issue.

If i could give more than 5 stars I seriously would. This is by far the best caravan park I've ever been to. It has a few BBQ areas, 2 impressive playgrounds with a flying fox, a pool, a water-play area, a jumping pillow, an arcade room, a communal fire pit, as well as some gas heaters around outdoor tables. The best part though is their activities room where they host multiple events throughout each day like movies, bingo, trivia, and kids activities like cooking and crafts classes. You could easily stay here for days without leaving the campsite. It's a nice big campsite that's well kept, full of trees, bright green grass and modern clean buildings. And as an added bonus, its beach side.

Fantastic place to stay, clean and wonderful facilities. Pool was clean but a bit cold. Caravan sites are big and concrete which was great. Really accommodating for children. Really one of the best parks we have found. Even in school holidays it was quiet and with facilities spread out it didn’t feel busy. Everything was well sign posted and it just felt like a well oiled machine. Highly recommend.

What good is having security just driving around and never hearing anything that's screamed literally every night for hours on end by a long stay lady in a tent?
And what good is the security man tonight that is driving towards me and can't miss seeing me long before he passes only 1.5 meters away from me, while waving for him to stop to be able to talk to him about the screaming that's still going on at 02.40am?! He looked me in the eyes and just drove past.
Nice comfy place

This park is a kids park. Like mc donalds drive thru/disneyland with sites. Their focus is on families and kids. So if you are a traveller who wants a peacefully relaxing holiday, this is not the park. Loud screaming kids until 10pm  thats the rules!!! They should have a designated area for people with kids, but they focus on families and kids, so DONT BOOK their NON REFUNDABLE WINTER EOFY sale thinking its going to be a nice enjoyable time outside of school holidays. I wont recommend this park to anyone that doesnt have kids.
Unreal holiday park, the biggest one I’ve ever seen with direct beach access. 10/10
Great park. Great facilities

Disappointing Stay – Unsafe Conditions and Poor Service

We stayed in Cabin 9 (Jimmy the Jellyfish) over the long weekend and were really let down by both the conditions and the service. The area outside our cabin was flooded when we arrived, making access difficult and soaking our shoes. A child in our group even slipped trying to avoid the water—clearly a safety hazard.

We raised the issue multiple times, but staff were dismissive and unhelpful. It wasn’t until the fourth complaint that maintenance pumped the water away, but the flooding returned. We had to create our own path with pavers just to get in and out.

We’ve stayed at many parks around South Australia and have never had an experience this poor. Even the cleaners for the cabin next door refused to go through the water, yet we were left to deal with it for three nights. We were with four other families who all agreed they wouldn’t return. Disappointing from a venue we expected much more from.

The manager, who was also acting as the maintenance worker, told us to raise our concerns after our stay via email and acknowledged that compensation would be the only option available, as there were no alternative cabins during our stay. We did this however have not had any reply from West Beach Parks!
Stayed in our own caravan so can’t really comment on the cabins.  Still a great park and fantastic facilities for kids! Even has a quiet play room for kids with autism etc
Great park, great staff!!

Great location for caravans. We hopped on the bus to the city (free for seniors) two days in a row and viewed the main buildings and a wonderful walk around the botanical gardens. We also hopped on the city loop bus to the market. We were spoilt for choice for lunch but I can assure you the Porchetta roll is to die for. Showers outside allowed for a KI Gin tasting before the bus to the caravan park.

Amazing facilities and really lovely service, the cabins had everything you need for to enjoy the time and share with the family, but at the same time it had enough space to sleep with enough privacy.
Love this joint.

We stayed over the Anzac day long weekend.  We had 2 x 3 bedroom cabins as there were 12 all together including grandparents, children & grandchildren. We all had a great time. Cabins were clean, modern & had everything that we needed. The grandies were never bored with the playground, pool, billiards, basketball etc. All staff were friendly & informative.  Thankyou for a fun weekend

Some of the staff are exceptionally Rude!!!!!
I’m sorry to write a bad review
Did not stay at the park but Stopped for breakfast.   Food was tasty and service was good.  Staff very friendly.
The most expensive caravan park I paid ever, and even, wi-fi not working.

There was a mix up with our booking and we were overcharged. Spoke with Delaney in the office - she was amazing!
Sorted the problem and arranged our refund without any trouble.
Thank you for your warm friendly service!
Much appreciated,

Can’t check in before 12pm for sites - non negotiable so don’t bother turning up before then. I was turned away at 11:30am and told to park up somewhere on Military Rd and come back.
Came back just before 12pm and there was already around 8 or more other campers waiting and as that increased it caused the traffic to stop on Military Rd! There was around 8 of us standing in reception waiting to check in and the staff waited until 12:02pm to start checking people in.. Absolutely ridiculous when you’ve got traffic flow issues and no real reason to not let campers on site.

To make matters worse, our site hadn’t been mowed and there was empty alcohol cans on it and rubbish from the previous people!

The airport is right here.. Planes take off over the park and fly out from 6am - 10pm which I hadn’t seen really advertised anywhere. It’s noisy as you can imagine.

The beach is right out the back fence of the park with a great boardwalk and footpath for walking or riding. The West Beach Surf Life Saving Club is a short stroll with great food.

The kiosk doesn’t take cash (which blows my mind) and I saw a lot of kids very disappointed when they had cash in their hands - maybe something to reconsider! Infact one staff member directed a young boy to reception to purchase an icy pole..
Coffee & hot chocolate was really nice though!

Overall, not really impressed with the park and the rudeness of staff over the phone (if answered) prior to arrival.
Lucky I dealt with a lovely young man at reception on check in.

Would we travel over from Victoria again to stay here? Probably not.
Fantastic park, which caters to every imaginable combination of travellers. It is truly has a remarkable array of guests' activities and events.

Fantastic park with great facilities,but lock your things up with really good locks.
We had our bikes stolen in the middle of the night,even though they were locked up.
Caravan Park is great location and has a beach front
Our favourite place to stay
I was just visiting a friend staying there  but I was impressed, it has everything and the surrounding area has all the activities you need for the kids
THIS PARK IS AMAZING BEST PLACE I HAVE STAYED ON MY TRIP LOCATION IS GREAT ROOM IS GREAT AND SERVICE IS AMAZING 5⭐️
Pretty hard to find any faults this place is incredible
What a wonderful place. The staff were friendly when we arrived. Told us everything we need to know.  Lots of amenities and activities. Our campsite is nice as well. Recommended.

When all other Adelaide parks are totally booked out this one is half full.  Why we ended up here unfortunately. Have had two amenities blocks closed at once. Pool closed one afternoon. Staff are not interested and openly grumbling about management   Disguising ducks constantly harassing our annex area.
The beach is currently having sand dumping so very limited access to the beach.   Large trucks so even walking on whats left of the beach is challenging
Permanents leaving at 5am for work.
Generally not enjoyable camping park.
Very secure kid and pet friendly park bring your van or just hire a space the beach is just out the back and the boat ramp is where you might spot a fish or two.

Great location, great facilities, but a 2 night minimum on a powered site when there are vacant lots available? Seems like an interesting business choice and excludes potential guests who might want to fill a vacant site last minute. Understood if perhaps single night bookings were only made available in the last few days before a weekend to ensure you maximise occupancy, but as a rule for non-long weekends in Autumn? Crazy.
We stayed here three times on our trip around SA. Lots to do, good location, lovely staff, I got some mail sent here while I was on KI. Highly recommend
Great family friendly caravan park. Everything you could need for a family getaway. Added bonus of being right by the beach 🫶

We stayed at West Beach Park for 19 nights and WOW! We've been 3 years fulltime travelling and this park is in our top 3 of Oz! So much for the kids!! Shout out to Deb, who does all activities at the park. Deb took us to the Golf Range, highly recommend! Kids absolutely loved Deb, her positivity, happy and welcoming smile brightened everyone's day. What an asset Deb is to the park!
We won't forget our time at West Beach.
Great park just too many people have n this occasion.
Great spot for camping with family. So much to do!
Quite good

Have been two weeks at this very popular caravan park. It's big! Was booked out for LIV golf wkd.Its a bit less busy at end of Feb but still people are coming and going. Has everything! Though the pool has been closed for a couple days. But plenty more for kids. Feb is better than Jan on pocket!
Overall a great place to stay in Adelaide.

I love staying here myself and so do my customers..the park is clean, well maintained and the cabin that we stayed in was absolutely beautiful. Great park for those with caravans and easy access for tour pick-ups. Highly recommended Gerry (All About Adelaide Tours)

Fantastic park!
By far the best we’ve been too in Australia, great for kids and adults.

Heaps to do and great coffee from the cafe.
Way better than expected in all aspects. There's a great indoor BBQ area called the Hub. Plenty of playgrounds spread around for kids. Great pool.

We used this caravan park for a hospital trip. The park has amazing facilities for kids and families, including a massive pool, great kids playground, jumping pillow, tennis and basketball ball courts and much more. We highly recommend this park with everything on offer and the beach at your back door.

While we were happy with the customer service and the quality of the cabin, the one bedroom in our cabin (Daphne the Dolphin) backed right on to the fence line of the major road (Military Road) and the loud, awful, drag racing traffic kept us awake ALL night. Even our digital clock radio switched loudly to white noise plus an extra pillow over my head did not help in any way. We gave up on any thought of sleep by 3am and were subsequently very early to our flight, and VERY tired for the big day ahead.

Great park with plenty of things for the kids
Staff at the front desk were very helpful when I had car issues. Great sunsets from the beach. While there be sure to visit the local surf club
Amazing beachfront caravan park!! So much for the kids to do. Highly recommend!
Great for kids and adults
Christmas eve 11pm had to change rooms due to finding bed bugs on one of the bunk beds which was hard with 3 kids and trying to get them asleep before Christmas

Caravan park feels more like a jail than a holiday park.
Kids are screaming 24/7 but if you’re an adult having a laugh you’ll get told off.
We stayed in a cabin the beds were rock hard shower pressure was really poor but fans were great. There is constant wiper snippets going and lawn mowers going every single morning at 7:30am Also planes are very loud here there is also no view of the beach. Internet also sucks same with 5G

Rooms were average
Great park, heaps to do with the kids
Location central which was good.
Great pool, cabin was a little  older but comfortable,  great location

We stayed here for a "Staycation" (as we live in Adelaide) to watch the Adelaide Giants Baseball for the weekend. Stayed in Willy the Whiting cabin for 1 night and Ted the Turtle for 3 nights.
Fantastic facilities...
Great for the kids, and the kids at heart... They truly cater for everyone...

We had a great experience here over 3 nights with one in a Willy Whiting cabin and 2 using our own caravan. The amenities for kids are amazing! Loved the splash park and playground for my 2 year old and mum and him also loved the strawberry and icecream truck on the Friday night too. It has a great reputation for a reason. They really do offer more than any other park. Would highly rate and will go back again one day.

Great park for kids but if you don't have them and want privacy and a bit of peace and quiet this is not the place for you. Not the caravan parks fault but people constantly walk through your site all day and night even when you are sitting across your site to prevent this! Where has camping etiquette gone? I do think the park needs to erect signs asking people to use assigned pathways as there are plenty of them leaving people absolutely no need to walk through sites, it's just pure laziness! Won't be back unfortunately
great park, very professional, close to everything Great

Good location to check out Adelaide with our camper trailer but feel the extra we paid to have an ensuite site was not justified.
We stayed 11 nights, the ensuite was cleaned once, the shower water pressure was terrible..I let the team know and they said they'd look at it - the pressure remained the same and we never received any feedback. We had high hopes of this park and it has good facilities for kids but the ensuite suite is not value for money.
Absolutely brilliant
Great family holiday!

This is a great little park with plenty of activities for the kids to do. In a great location close to the beach and Glenelg. The male staff member at the shack needs some customer service training though. The cabins are fine, possibly need a freshen up.

Had some legal documentation to be sent ASAP.
Staff were incredibly obliging and understanding.
This is on top of an excellent park to stay and enjoy.
Thankyou Suzanne !
Leeroy loves this park been living here for a couple of years now
Lovely clean, family friendly park. Amenities are good. Lots for kids to do, and close to the beach, Glenelg, great location.

New area for kids to learn the road safety.

Need parents to monitor the kids to avoid any bike or scooter accident.
To crowded, even not in School Holidays
Amazing caravan park, one of the best I've been to. So many inclusive activities for the kids and parents. Well worth the cost. There was even public transport to the city right outside the park.

Very close the beach, it is good idea with family trip.
I really like. Highly recomend this accommodation.
Best park in Adelaide
Nice area near the beach. Had boomgate troubles and a kind young male worker called seeked assistance for me.
Beautiful spot! Continued to work for us.

I recently asked at the reception desk if I could be let in to have a look at the park before making a booking.
The receptionist was extremely rude, she cut me off before I had finished my sentence. She said it's all online and that I was not allowed in. Very poor customer service. I won't be staying here and will not be recommending it to anyone.
The staff got really angry at me and my friend because we were running on the jumpy pillow
Staff are extremely helpful and the park is pristine

Wow. What a location. The park was amazing lots of things to entertain the kids even special events and movie night. The facilities in general are fantastic bbqs, laundry, kitchen area etc. Staff were super friendly and very helpful. Plenty of things to keep you busy but if you want other places to go the road safety school, mini golf and the beach are walking distance.
Only thing that was a little annoying was the sprinklers on at night sprayed on the footpath so if you need to go to the toilets you just have to accept getting wet. This was so minor and actually would have loved if it was summer lol. I only mention it as i can imagine some people being very annoyed by it that.
Highly recommend. We will definitely be back.
We had an excellent stay here. They have so many activities for young and old. We were very happy with our stay on a powered site. Generous sized sites too which are great for a good set up

Facilities great and superb location, unfortunately EVERY interaction we had with front desk staff was unpleasant or disappointing to the point that we won’t ever go back. I don’t want to deal with grumpy people, especially on my holiday! The second you walk in that reception area it’s clear they’re not happy and were very quick to be unhelpful and snappy.  Also (not their fault) OMG the planes at 6am are crazy loud!!

I would choose somewhere else for the money!
Great family location
Excellent park close to beach,it has everything you could need or want.

First time at this park and stayed in a cabin. Can't fault the park facilities or the kid friendliness. We even did cookie decorating, which was free. The kids had a blast.
I'm unsure of value for money for the cabins though, it is pretty ordinary, and there is no shampoo/conditioner or body wash in the bathroom which I wasn't aware of. Couldn't wait to wash my hair when I got home!
We will be back someday, but maybe just on a powered site on ensuite site to save some money.

This park has a massive range of activities, and the cabins are nice and simple. The carpet is nice and the beds are very comfortable. There is no bedside in the children’s room, and the windows are not clean. There are lots of power points, and the blu ray disc is ok. Overall nice little place to stay.
ALSO the car park is unbelievably small for a V8 Land Cruiser!
The premium ensuite site is the best location wise since it is right on the beach front. Overall a well maintained park with good facilities.

How sad it is the park has dropped its standards. I will not return.
Filthy mains tap water
Some showers no hooks to hang up your towel only a 10cm wide shelf
Staff that are obliviously blind or just don’t do there job properly
I just stayed five days unable to sit outside as people from adjoining sites are allowed to park their car over your site. Every single day we stayed the Ute was over the yellow painted site divider  and when I went to the office I was told not their problem up to me to sort the neighbour out
Good thinking office staff then when I go out the stupid neighbour who is unable to park their car probably would run into my caravan.
Now I guess you will have some desk jockey public relations person type a reply trying to make me look silly so just for that person I have included pics.
Maybe you could use the desk jockey to help people that are unable to park to show them how to park on their site.
Also no body I saw takes notice of your speed display signs. Everyone I saw was speeding. I am guessing that’s what the red flashing numbers and the frowning face show.
I think you need to employ staff that have some sort of experience with caravan parks. Not just a uni grad who has read a book about a caravan park.
A boy named Lenny was very nice and helpful when I asked for directions around the park. Overall very nice community and facilities.
So much to do for all ages clean amenities and grounds great little shop that sold the best hot chips and coffee friendly staff a fantastic place to holiday 😀
This is our of the best parks we have stayed at.
What a gem, fantastic park, great location, very clean amenities, wonderful facilities for kids Definitely recommend this park.
Suits our purpose to be here!
Great family location. Kids have so many activities to do.
Expensive for power and water site but close to Adelaide city

Stayed here twice in 2021 in a cabin. The cabins were clean, but nothing special. It’s close to the beach and airport, so you can go plane spotting if you’re into that which I am.

However, there’s a pool and gaming room which I especially loved. There was also a strawberry and pancake stand, and I went to the movie night!
Nice and central and on the beach. Great boardwalk along the beach. Great bike riding up and down the beach on bike track. 15 kilometres to Adelaide oval on bike track.

Service from reception is everything being the first point of contact when you come into a park. The older blonde receptionist was very rude & when we told them the name of our relative who’s onsite van we were staying in, she decided to tell us that “nobody is named that” and was very defensive all because we weren’t 100% sure on the location of our site & the names we were giving weren’t matching our site number. We are here for medical reasons so an ugly conversation with a receptionist is very off putting when already dealing with other issues. Definitely suggest this receptionist learns how to communicate properly & nicely with guests - as a receptionist myself.
Great park heaps for kids to do and awsome camp kitchen
Lovely clean Caravan Park.  Right on the beach and handy to public transport, shops and airport.  Fun attractions for the kids.
Friendly staff
Lovely park spotless clean

Came Here Today With Family
Excellent Customer Service
Excellent Prices

An ideal holiday experience, for all types of families, couples, or single travellers.
They even had yoga 🧘‍♂️ classes, in amongst there vast variety of modern facilities.
Love the park it great for family and we stay in  our motorhome plenty things to do for Kids and the Big kids at heart ❤️

The park is full of great activities for the kids, from playgrounds and jumping pillows to swimming pools and even a games room for when the weather outside isn't the greatest. They also put on a lot of activities in the holidays, like dancing and taekwondo. The park is also on the beach, with access via a couple of gates. There is an on-site kiosk that sells many things you might want or need from milk to alcohol. We stayed in a cabin, which was great, although the beds were a little hard, but that would be the only complaint I have. Staff were fantastic.
Great place to stay

Great caravan park. We used to stay on  powered sites but we don't anymore as we have a little dog  now and they have a no dog policy. I wish they'd reconsider their policy. Even capping the number of dogs allowed in the park at one time or outside of school holidays or a section of the park for dog owners. I've never met a badly behaved dog camping. If they are, the owners leave them home.
Well equipped room. Kids playing areas are wonderful. Every thing is good except that we need scan the card to get into toilet, inconvenient for kids at all.
What an amazing location and facilities, went to visit grandson and he had a ball of a time on the park facilities, great desert Van, and even shop great for breakfast at entrance/exit…10/10

We always love our Easter family holiday here.
This year some small disappointments with some run down facilities. Hope to see improvements next visit.

It's no secret why this park wins awards. It's on the beach, has everything you need. Sausage sizzle, wine tasting, coffee, hot snacks, live music, everything to keep kids amused, welcoming staff & spotless amenities. But once in the gate, it seems no one is responsible for safety. In good weather the pool was packed, with teens up deep end bombing & in shallow end playing touch footy, the parents in the middle clutching children for safety. There is a sign "no lifeguard on duty" covers the parks' responsibility, I think,  but surely in busy times a staff member should be present in the pool area. There was alcohol, with a man even bringing in his esky. The younger children (12ish?) were able to step on concrete base of the pool fence & over into pool area because they were unable to open the gate. All this in front of a 'security' man who stood outside the area talking to someone he obviously knew, for at least 40 mins. There were Escooters, hoverboards, 'sitewalkers' too lazy to take a path preferring to step on people's power cords & hoses. This included kids on bikes. This was mentioned to the office & "it will be noted". Instead I got 6 texts in one day with the same message "1 car per site" people never took much notice of that either. Having said all of that, would we return? Absolutely, its beautiful. but never ever again in a holiday period. It was too unpleasant.
Great caravan park,good cabins,camping areas and close to the beach great for families

本来有点担心，离机场太近。结果并没有那么吵，可能是飞机起降的方向并没有朝向这边。

Powersite还可以，水龙头有些漏水需要维护了。Ensuite相比就有点差强人意，设施有点老了，热水洗澡还可以，马桶就不咋地。不过园区规划还不错，公共浴室和厕所都挺干净。

这个Holiday park真的超多玩的东西，女儿玩的超级开心！沙滩也很漂亮，日落每天都可以去看。

What a great park plenty to do with the kids
At least two places to get food and coffee
Very big park very clean and tidy
Visited friends who were staying at the caravan park.
Like a 5 star hotel for caravan and camping.
Nice campsite and good location on the beach. New playgrounds recently added for the kids.

This is easily one of the best caravan parks we've experienced.  It's the only caravan park that we've stayed in that can genuinely be called a resort. It has a great check-in process, with 3 parking lanes and a large, well staffed reception area. The toilet blocks are plentiful,  spacious, and pretty well equipped. They're probably 4-star in an otherwise 5-star caravan park. Whist we were there they were only cleaned once a day, which wasn't sufficient for the use they were getting, particularly from children. There was plenty of nice green lawn area all over the park, which would have been a challenge for the army of maintenance staff, considering the dry conditions. This would be the perfect caravan park for anyone with kids. There are playgrounds everywhere as well as a great pool and separate splash park. There are also two cafes onsite, one open to 8pm, serving coffee, and takeaway food.
Best of all (for us), the park is located right on a beautiful swimming beach and walking/bike path that extends for tens of kilometres in both directions. We were able to walk all the way to the Adelaide  CBD without getting off walking trails (about 15kms one-way). The walk south to Glenelg, and beyond, is also great.

West Beach Park is amazing. So many facilities, so many activities.
Its a beautiful and clean park.
Most definitely the best park for a staycation in Adelaide

Regular theft at the park. 🚨
We had an expensive bike stolen from within the park. Upon talking to SA Police we were told this is a regular occurrence at this caravan park. The staff were slow to react to our concerns and despite being a ‘secure park’ they had no CCTV footage of the bikes (2 stolen that night) leaving the park. We spent hours trying to get staff to assist us by checking footage as well as dealing with SA Police and posting to locals pages to try to locate the stolen bike which could have been spent enjoying our stay at the park.
The lack of empathy was upsetting and they wouldn’t even give us a refund for the inconvenience caused.
Ensure you lock up all belongings during your stay.

Otherwise the park is great for families and a great location.

Kid's school came here for camp this year. Great place, they've spent a lot of effort into it, and it shows. Beach literally over the hill, great staff. Planning to sort out a holiday ourselves this year from what I saw second hand.

It was a great place to take the family. Lots of activities for the family, and water park or pool options. Close access to the beach also. Great sites, facilities cleaned and maitained well. Really impressed for a first visit
Great staff always greeted with a positive outlook and awesome smile. It shows how well a team can run with great staff and managers, customers always have amazing things to say about the whole place
Bit more expsensive than others but the facilities are better and they have a lot of things for families to do within the park itself
Excellent utilities and rooms are not that far from the pool. I will recommend this for anyone that is looking for a get away for a couple of nights.

Very good camping but expensive. Pretty quiet. All facilities are clean and huge. There is a laundry. WiFi is not working everywhere on the camp.. 4G reception not working. Unpowered site little bit small.
It is a camping.
Perfect location for a family getaway.

A great stay every time.2023
Stayed again this year.😁2024
Had a power outage on our site yesterday afternoon .The service staff were so prompt and professional.  The complex power  issue was investigated,  resolved quickly and communication was excellent. Thanks, West beach big 4 team.  Such great service and park features.

Great location, good amenities for kids, access to beach, on-site bbq's (not all were working).
I do think it's expensive for what it is, but I guess that's Australia for you 🤷🏽‍♀️

Great location, nice big sites. Relaxed feel, great for kids.. school holiday activities are great. All staff have been super nice.
Would highly recommend to families
thanks to cj for giving us a great fun time while at our stay - he is so kind and allowed us to have a fabulous time!! his service was impeccable!! lots of love - midnight swimmers ❤️❤️
The 'Shack will not accept cash i chose not to purchase anything on principle so if you do just keep on eye if any surcharges
Very great places to stay
We have stayed here 4 times and we love it . Thanks to Camp Quality we have had a staycation when my son needed it most 💙

Beautiful caravan park nestled near the beach.
The only thing I could say is I wish the cheaper cabins had a toilet for the kids.
Is Eric Klein very well? Looked after staff is fantastic and I go out of their way for you very well recommended. Definitely five star plus
Amazing for the kids and parents too. Highly recommend
The west beach big 4 is really good I think it’s the best big 4.

10/10 outstanding customer service by anna-lena and a young male with brown hair (didn’t catch his name was working in the kiosk) my son has Autism and they offered so much support and sorted out our problems quick smart. Very kind and understanding.

One of the best caravan parks we’ve stayed at. Awesome service, clean facilities and fantastic kids program. Don’t really need to leave the park once you’re there! Kids had the best week ever. Would definitely recommend.

Had 5 nights here Nov 2023. Lovely camp, nice grassy pitch with power and water. Great location to explore the coast to Glenelg or in to Adelaide City. A bus can be caught outside the camp and goes to Adelaide in around 45 mins or 10 mins to Glenelg then you can get a tram to the city. Very helpful camp staff, beach access only yards away and spotless showers and toilets.

I am just reporting that the dump point along 4th Avenue has been faulty since about the 9th of November and has been reported several times with nothing being done about it. It is a disgusting mess and a health hazard and needs to be fixed now or I will report it to the council as a health hazard. I know there is another one in the park but I cannot walk that far with a cassette
Great place and location. Great facilities
Heaps for the kids to do. No real need to go anywhere.

The cabins are good and cozy the only problem is that’s ants, lots of it. You need to clean and wash your dishes all the time and make sure no sweets are open in any bags. Far from that service and location are very good 😊

This was our first time staying at a big 4. Just the 2 adults. What an amazing place!!!! We will DEFINITELY be booking here with our kids for Xmas hopefully if we can secure a room. For anyone wanting to know we stayed in Ted the turtle room and for $100 a night can’t complain. Awesome pressure in the shower, bed was comfy, great aircon! Bloody brilliant. Thanks again guys
We had a beautiful day spent with family!! Lots to do! Face painting, play grounds, pool, dessert pop up stand, kiosk, Cafe. Live music was a Bonus & Ben was really good!! It's a great spot. 👍

Great place, friendly staff, clean & tidy.
So much for kids to do, hot pool, beach very close. Fun park next door & mini golf. Kids will love it.
Only down side its close to airport, can get alittle noisy at night. We had a van, so not sure about the cabins.
But overall we would  come back to stay.
I am currently staying in a Jimmy the Jellyfish cabin for nights. Comfortable and clean, and very close to the beach. Will stay here again.

Very good for family's with all aged children they had a free movie showing for family's.
We have our own self contained van so only used water and power. Looked good. 👍

Made reservations over phone with a girl named ally she was very helpful and made sure our caravan site was close as possible with our friends that booked there as well.
Her personality was bubbly, friendly, polite so professional at doing her job. Will definitely ask for her when we next book again.

Stayed 2 nights powered sites. Kids had a good time.
Would benefit from a better water play area. Seems a little behind the times compared to other places we have stayed. But staff friendly, well kept grounds and would recommend for people with kids up to about 12 years old. Very big park.
Fun for the whole family!
Nice park . Clean and well kept. Convenient shop on site but food is terrible

It was very disappointing that the water park was under construction.
We only booked to be able to use the water park. They could have told us before but they kept it secret and still charged full price

Loved the cabin & pool, very nice park.
Very comfortable beds,
The bed in the cabin we occupied was the worst one in many years of travelling.

Disappointing.
This park felt like a let down from the get go. We arrived and checked in at 4.30pm on a Saturday, set up our van and then headed out to the supermarket. Turns out in Adelaide these shut at 5pm on the weekend ?
I feel like this is very helpful information to give to interstate travelers when checking in.

The toilets were all broken in some way or another (locks / lids/ holders) and were filthy all day every day. They are cleaned once per day , first thing in the morning but with so many visitors it’s impossible for one clean to be enough .

The park has lots of recreational options for kids , but for a park this size there is no where near enough, and all are a huge walk away if your site is not central . The pool and jumping pillow were packed from the The minute they open until the minute they close.

The movie every night is not always family friendly (which wouldn’t be an issue except that in 5 days we were there every movie was m15 so unfortunately couldn’t enjoy this even though we were all excited for this ! there is no way to tell before you lugg your chair and family down to the start of the movie.

No kids, so don't care about jumping pillows and similar. Would be great if you had kids though, there are plenty of friends running around. It is expensive but then it's close to Adelaide...  However expensive and lack of maintenance is another thing, most of their washing machines don't work and nothing appears to being done to fix this. Their showers are also amongst the worst I have experienced as the hot water is very low pressure so a gusher shower head is basically a dribbler.  Also there are no NRV so if you turn the cold on to a normally reasonable amount, you can expect the hot water pipes to fill up with cold water which I would assume would confuse most people trying to adjust the taps for a desirable temperature.

WI-FI may as well not be connected.  Why is it so hard to get wifi in a caravan Park??? At worst it interferes with your mobile data so you have to switch wifi off. At best your device simply doesn't register there is any wifi so carries on a normal on mobile data.  This meant that we couldn't have our movie nights we'd planned.  The movie that was put on by the park was impossible to hear so we gave up on that too.
We stayed in a Larry lobster cabin - it's very nice and was spotlessly clean. The couch and beds were comfy.
The pool was just warm enough to be able to get into however my skinny 8yo was still freezing within 15 minutes.  I'm sure I read that it was heated to 28°, I don't think it was while we were there.
It's a very big park (especially if you're right siren one end like we were) and you have to leave the park to buy food so bear this in mind if you have younger children.
My kids had a great time on the bouncy pillow and in the pool and it was nice to be at a beachside caravan Park where the beach is actually close by and not still a 20 minute trek.
Overall I'm grateful that my middle child refused to come because there would have been a meltdown over the lack of wifi given it said there is wifi.
I don't think we'd stay here again as we live in Adelaide so really we want the park itself to be entertaining.  I think I'd go to the one next door next time because it has waterslides...
It was nice to have a short get away with 2 of my kids before one moves away.
Very good apart from the irons to iron your clothes the irons are all black and been like that for 2 years or more.
Nice and clean. Great price as well
Great service
Been going there for  years  lovely  place

Would well and truly recommend this place. We had a 5 night stay and this park well and truly met our needs. Nice and close to the airport and far enough away from the CBD that the hustle and bustle are near non existent. Except for the park being demolished (which we were made aware of well before arrival) that was the only let down. The kids were still well amused with the 2 jumping pillows, beach just out the gates and pool. Although i do wish the pools on both sides were hated to the same heat. Only downfalls were the bed was a bit hard for my liking BUT that comes down to personal comfort and feeling and the airport being so close but because of the airport curfew it doesn’t overly bother you much. All in all a great park. If i done another trip to Adelaide would definitely stay here again.

Such a immaculately clean place, very well maintained, lawns cut, huge clean laundry room…All bathroom facilities and dump points  clean.  Can’t fault this place.
If I could give this place 10 ⭐️ I would!!!!

Fantadtic park, very secure, we had a caravan site with concrete pad, the amenities were always clean, heated pool in winter, small gym, kids activity room, 1 minute & your feet are in the sand. A bus stop out front to ride you into town, 2 onsite cafes, alcohol for sale, coin & card swipe washing machines. 40 minute walk to eithe Henley or Glenelg Beach.  A great park, and only 10 minutes to the airport.
Fantastic Caravan/Tourist park
Five Stars for a five star holiday park. Absolutely fantastic park, facilities are immaculate, everything well maintained and extremely secure. Staff are friendly.
Great cabins

Stayed in a 3 bedroom house,first disappointment not 1 coat-hanger,  fridge seal not working all the time so alarm sounding, 3 pillows in the main bedroom no extras or blankets in the cupboards considering winter time and a few minor issues, they should do a walk through

I loved it there, so much to do, took the grandkids and they we're swimming in the heated pool 8:30 in the morning and when we were leaving asked if we could come back for a week I'll be back for sure 😊
Great family park. Booked in advance so rooms were cheap. Pool even heated enough kids could swim in July! Beach access amazing.

Park always rates highly. Stayed here previously and service, facilities and location makes it our go to place in Adelaide. Live music on Sunday plus food van on some days are a bonus. Try the chicken satay on chips if you can. Easy bus into Glenelg or city connect. Highly recommended.

Spent a few days in our caravan there, they gave us a large site near toilets and close to beach exit.
They don’t allow dogs but were very happy to greet my Assistance Dog

Good but would have appreciated some assistance finding our cabin upon arrival. It was dark, cold, windy & raining and as we didn't arrive in a vehicle ( got dropped at reception by a taxi ), we had to haul all of our luggage around the park looking for our cabin. There was cars coming up behind us in the dark so we were also trying to avoid the cars. The cabin was a true blessing when we finally found it.
Great location,  facilities and security.
Very nice site, all the bells and whistles
Very good place to stay for a few nights.
Lovely and quiet park with very friendly & helpful staff members. We will be returning in 2024.

Large park close to city - unfortunately their huge price increase (44%) for a long weekend is a rip off - I know staff have to be looked after but an extra $20 per night for a site is ridiculous - I'm willing to bet the majority of this increase goes straight into management's pocket and not towards the deserving staff...... hopefully they put it towards updating the outdated amenity blocks - maybe start by fixing all the leaking taps ....

My daughter & her family stayed here.
They do it every year for a week long sporting event.
Nice clean park on the beach good spot
Lovely park but staff could have been nicer not that helpful
Central to everything!
